title: Soft, Flaky Russian Bread Pilaf
description: A comforting Russian dish featuring malty, flaky bread and tomato juice, making it a great fodmap-friendly option.

ingredients.name: Day old bread, cut into 1-inch cubes

instructions: Toss the bread cubes with butter until evenly coated, then spread on a baking sheet.
Bake the bread in the preheated oven for 10 minutes, or until lightly toasted.
In a large saucepan, sauté the chopped onion and minced garlic over medium heat until softened.
Add the tomato juice, dried dill weed, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan, and stir to combine.
Add the toasted bread cubes to the saucepan, and stir to coat with the tomato juice mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, uncovered, for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has been absorbed and the bread is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh dill if desired.
